title: Data Analyst
description:
In het kort
International Card Services (ICS) is een betalingsdienstverlener en marktleider op het gebied van het uitgeven van creditcards in Nederland. We zijn een 100% dochteronderneming van ABN AMRO Bank N.V. De financiële wereld verandert snel. Als toonaangevende speler in deze sector streven we ernaar om innovatie en duurzaamheid te combineren met stabiliteit en veiligheid. Om onze positie verder te versterken en bij te dragen aan onze missie, zijn we op zoek naar een Data Analist die analytisch inzicht en technische expertise samenbrengt om data om te zetten in impactvolle oplossingen voor onze stakeholders binnen ICS.
Je werk
-    Creëren van rapportages en dashboards waarmee o.a. financiële processen, onboarding en klantinteracties gemonitord kunnen worden en relevante KPI’s inzichtelijk worden gemaakt. Je zorgt ervoor dat stakeholders altijd toegang hebben tot actuele en bruikbare informatie.
-    Verzamelen, structureren en analyseren van grote datasets gericht op het verkrijgen van diepgaande inzichten in bijvoorbeeld klantgedrag en operationele efficiëntie, met als doel het verbeteren van processen en klanttevredenheid.
-    Vertalen van complexe data-inzichten naar concrete, begrijpelijke adviezen voor management en stakeholders. Je rapporteert bijvoorbeeld over trends in klantgedrag, financiële- en operationele processen, en geeft aanbevelingen voor optimalisering gebaseerd op jouw analyses.
-    Samenwerken met de andere teams binnen en buiten de Data Office om data-analyses en inzichten te vertalen naar praktische toepassingen binnen projecten gericht op het verbeteren van onboarding, klantbehoud en financiële processen.
-    Uitvoeren van data-validatie en kwaliteitscontroles om ervoor te zorgen dat analyses gebaseerd zijn op nauwkeurige en betrouwbare gegevens.
Werkomgeving
Je komt te werken in het Data Office, het kloppend hart van onze data gedreven transformatie. Vijf teams werken hier dagelijks aan één missie: data inzetten als strategisch vermogen. Het Data Office bestaat onder andere uit een Data Product Management Team, een Data Hub, een AA & AI team en twee Reporting teams. Je zal komen te werken in het Business Reporting team. Dit team is verantwoordelijk voor alle MI (Management Informatie) met betrekking tot Onboarding, Collections, Sales & Marketing, Finance en het Customer Contact Center.
We staan aan het begin van een transitie naar een meer gecentraliseerde en toekomstbestendige dataomgeving. Als data analist speel je een belangrijke rol in het implementeren van gecentraliseerde datamodellen die organisatiebreed gebruikt worden. Daarnaast werk je actief mee aan het rationaliseren en vereenvoudigen van onze bestaande rapportages. Je helpt ons om van versnipperde inzichten naar uniforme en schaalbare informatievoorziening te gaan zodat besluitvorming sneller, consistenter en meer datagedreven wordt.
We zijn een jong en ambitieus team die dagelijks onze business helpt om meer datagedreven te worden en zoeken iemand die het leuk vindt om hier aan bij te dagen.
Je profiel
-    Een afgeronde bachelor- of masteropleiding in een relevant werkgebied. Of een gelijkwaardig denk- en oplossend niveau in een kwantitatief georiënteerd vakgebied.
-    Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in data-analyse binnen de financiële sector, consultancy of een vergelijkbare omgeving. Kennis van de financiële sector is een pré.
-    Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring in een data-analistfunctie met PBI (DAX-codering) en SQL.
-    Ruime ervaring met het gebruiken van data-analyse om besluitvorming te beïnvloeden en strategische initiatieven te leiden, waarbij je effectief samenwerkt met stakeholders om inzichten om te zetten in actie..
-    Sterke vaardigheden in stakeholdermanagement, waarbij je proactief en helder communiceert met interne en externe stakeholders om verwachtingen te beheren, draagvlak te creëren en gezamenlijke doelen te bereiken.
-    Ervaring met werken in een scrum/agile-omgeving.
-    Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden in zowel het Nederlands als Engels, in staat om complexe data-gedreven scenario’s duidelijk uit te leggen aan verschillende doelgroepen, inclusief senior management en cross-functionele teams, en om actief input van stakeholders te integreren in je analyses.
-    Toont eigenaarschap door verantwoordelijkheid te nemen voor eigen acties en beslissingen en zet zich in voor het behalen van (afdeling)doelen of het oplossen van problemen.
-    Je bent analytisch sterk, nieuwsgierig en niet bang om de status quo uit te dagen. Je verbindt mensen en data, stelt scherpe vragen en neemt initiatief om verbeteringen te realiseren, waarbij je stakeholders effectief meeneemt in de door jou voorgestelde veranderingen.
Wij bieden
-    Een hybride werkplek (minimaal 2 dagen in de week op ons kantoor in Diemen en de rest vanuit huis).
-    Een maandsalaris variërend van €3.706 tot €5.742 gebaseerd op een 36-urige werkweek, inclusief vakantiegeld en het Benefit budget. (We zoeken iemand met passie, drive en commitment om het verschil te maken. De functie is ingeschaald op niveau 8 tot en met 9, afhankelijk van ervaring, zelfstandigheid en de mate waarin je de brug weet te slaan tussen data, mensen en impact.)
-    Een benefit budget van 11% waarmee je flexibel arbeidsvoorwaarden kunt kopen.
-    Vijf weken vakantie per jaar. Je hebt de mogelijkheid om vier extra weken per jaar bij te kopen.
-    Een persoonlijk ontwikkelbudget van € 1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot  € 3.000.
-    Een jaarkaart voor het openbaar vervoer met gratis reizen door heel Nederland.
-    Een uitstekende pensioenregeling.
Interesse?
Ben je geïnteresseerd? Dan horen we graag van je! Reageer nu op deze vacature door je CV en motivatiebrief toe te voegen. Heb je nog vragen? Dan kan je contact met ons opnemen via lvdsijs@icscards.nl (06 20 66 25 50) of awillering@icscards.nl (06 33 01 10 84). We maken graag kennis met je!
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam

title: Capaciteitsmanager Business Clients & Ecosystems
description:
In het kort
Ben jij die ervaren Capaciteitsmanager die workforce management binnen onze afdeling naar een hoger niveau wil brengen? Ben je een ervaren WFM-er, slim, ambitieus, analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig?  Weet jij als geen ander hoe je interne stakeholders moet overtuigen van jouw adviezen en bevindingen? Dan gaan wij graag met jou in gesprek!
Je werk
Je werkt in een klein WFM team en bent daar verantwoordelijk voor de WFM cyclus en het forecastproces bij Business Clients. Je maakt de forecast voor alle werkstromen die met name via Microsoft Dynamics lopen en onderzoekt hoe we in de toekomst alle werkzaamheden van de business kunnen forcecasten en inplannen via WFM. Je stelt een capaciteitsplan op en geeft het management aan de hand hiervan advies over benodigde capaciteit en werkverdeling. De uitkomsten van het capaciteitsoverleg draag je over aan de Planners en Traffic managers. Je levert een proactieve bijdrage aan de inzet en optimalisatie van WFM. Je rapporteert aan de manager WFM BC&E. Het betreft een fulltime functie.
Werkomgeving
Bij het Centre of Expertise (CoE) WFM zijn alle WFM experts binnen de bank ondergebracht die voor verschillende afdelingen WFM neerzetten en uitvoeren. Je komt te werken in het executie team dat verantwoordelijk is voor WFM bij Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E).
Je standplaats wordt de Wisselwerking 58 in Diemen maar bij het Centre of Expertise WFM werken wij hybride, hierbij bepalen de werkzaamheden wat voor jou die dag de beste werkplek is.
Business Clients & Ecosystems
De client unit Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E) is verantwoordelijk voor het bedienen van zakelijke klanten met een omzet tot 25 miljoen Euro, ingedeeld in 'sectorclusters'. De focus van BC&E ligt op het behalen van onze groeidoelstelling om de nummer één bank voor het midden- en kleinbedrijf  (zakelijke klanten met omzet tot 25 miljoen) te worden.
BC&E bedient +/- 370.000 zakelijke klanten (inclusief Medical Business Clients). De afdeling adviseert zakelijke klanten over dagelijkse zakelijke producten en diensten, zoals leningen tot een miljoen Euro, betaaloplossingen, sparen, verzekeringen, beyond en near banking proposities en ondersteuning bij het dagelijks bankieren.
Je profiel
Meerdere jaren werkervaring op het gebied van Workforce management (WFM); Ervaring met het implementeren van WFM bij een nieuwe afdeling is een pre.
Analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig.
Kennis en ervaring met PowerBI rapportages is een pre.
Wij bieden
Het is voor ons belangrijk om een goede werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden je daarom een functie met impact én uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden die bijdragen aan jouw werkplezier, ontwikkeling en wellbeing.
Daarnaast bieden wij:
Een aantrekkelijk bruto maandsalaris op basis van een 36urige werkweek. Dit is inclusief vakantiegeld en een flexibel benefit budget.
Een uitstekende pensioenregeling, zodat je goed voorbereid bent voor later.
Flexibiliteit in werken: thuiswerken is mogelijk in overleg met je team en afhankelijk van je functie. Wij regelen een ergonomische thuiswerkplek voor je.
Ruimte voor ontspanning met vijf weken vakantie per jaar, aangevuld met twee verplichte vrije dagen. Daarnaast kun je jaarlijks tot vier extra weken vakantie bijkopen.
Vijf ‘Banking for better days’: extra vrije dagen die je kunt inzetten voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling of vrijwilligerswerk.
Persoonlijke ontwikkeling staat centraal: je ontvangt een ontwikkelbudget van €1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot €3.000.
Een OV-jaarabonnement met gratis openbaar vervoer in heel Nederland voor zakelijk en privé gebruik.
Interesse?
Heb je interesse? Reageer dan door middel van een sollicitatie. Voor extra inhoudelijke info graag even reageren naar
thomas.van.hassel@nl.abnamro.com
/ 06 1439 7379.
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam
Bij ABN AMRO geloven we in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Gelijke kansen voor iedereen vormen een belangrijk fundament, waarbij we streven naar een inclusieve cultuur waarin alle medewerkers zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelen. Onze visie als
persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd
sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. We richten ons op klantbeleving, duurzaamheid en bouwen aan een toekomstbestendige bank, waarbij we gelijke beloning voor gelijk werk jaarlijks onderzoeken om eerlijke kansen te waarborgen. Sluit je aan bij een bank die vindingrijkheid en ambitie omarmt, en maak samen met ons impact voor een betere toekomst.
Als je wilt solliciteren voor het B-Able of Reboot programma, zorg er dan voor dat je dit vermeldt zowel in de titel als in de inhoud van je cv.

title: GenAI Project Lead
description:
At a glance
Are you passionate about pioneering Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions that redefine customer experiences in the financial services industry? ABN AMRO’s AI team is seeking a visionary GenAI Project Lead to guide our efforts in leveraging AI across various domains in banking, including the creation of agentic AI solutions. As GenAI Project Lead, you’ll drive innovation, collaborate closely with business counterparts and champion the implementation of AI solutions that revolutionize the banking experience.
Your job
You’ll work on a portfolio of use cases that range from improving customer contact (e.g. voicebot) to revolutionizing the way how we interact with documents by leveraging GenAI. As an GenAI Project Lead you’ll be working with colleagues from the team on projects across the bank. We take care of the full development cycle from exploring and identifying opportunities, experimenting, and creating prototypes, to developing scalable products. Your activities include:
Lead projects in (Gen)AI, ensuring seamless integration and maximum impact.
Collaborate with entrepreneurs and business experts to ideate, design and implement AI solutions tailored to the address the unique challenges of our employees and customers.
Co-create AI use cases and business cases with counterparts and business lines or ventures, with a focus on improving customer engagements and satisfaction across all touchpoints.
Manage the vision, road map and backlog of AI initiatives to ensure a successful deployment and adoption across different areas of the bank.
Drive risk related processes to ensure compliant production and implementation of AI solutions within the bank.
Fostering culture of innovation and collaboration with the AI team empowering members to thrive and deliver exceptional results by mentoring and coaching junior and mid-level colleagues nurturing their growth and development within the AI domain.
Working environment
You’ll be part of the AI Team of the Innovation division in Amsterdam and we provide ample opportunities for hybrid working. We have a start-up vibe. Our team consists of people (25+) with a passion for AI and we work every day to create (measurable) impact for the bank. There is an open team atmosphere where initiative is encouraged, and we provide daily coaching for juniors in our teams.
Your profile
Proven track record in leading AI initiatives with a focus on transforming customer interactions (minimum 5 years).
Deep understanding of (Gen)AI technologies and their pratical applications.
Excellent communicator with the ability to engage and collaborate effectively with technical and business stakeholders.
Visionary leader who can drive innovation and navigate complex challenges to deliver tangible results.
Preference for candidate with startup or entrepreneurial experience along with familiarity working in agile environments.
Personality profile:
You have a sense for business, and understand how AI delivers tangible business value
You are pragmatic and value-focused, e.g. not afraid to work with existing solutions to reduce time-to-market
You are able to communicate effectively with technical and business counterparts
You are a teamplayer that requires minimal steering to be productive
You are up to date on the latest developments within AI and can guide others in finding optimal solutions
You have an entrepreneurial (product driven) mindset and are a self-starter.
We are offering
The opportunity to be the best you can be, work flexible hours and lots of room to grow both personally and professionally
A competitive yearly salary ranging from EUR 85.000 – EUR 120.000 all-in (including holiday allowance and benefit budget) annual salary based on 36 hours with the possibility to work 40 hours (pro-rated)
11% of the above salary is benefit budget, which you can spend on additional fringe benefits or paid out as general salary
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year
An public transportation card for both business and private use
A very solid pension plan
A variety of special leaves
Interested?
Ready to lead the charge in transforming customer interactions with AI? Apply now before April 9th and be part of our dynamic team!
Please note: Instead of a traditional motivational letter, we ask you to write your responses to the following questions:
Tell us about an AI project that you are proud of, what your role was in that project and how you managed the team to achieve the desired result.
Describe how an ideal working week would look for you as GenAI Project Lead at ABN AMRO.
What activities would you be doing, also point out activities that you wouldn’t like to be doing.
Describe a project or situation that you found yourself in where you had to achieve something difficult and where you had to reach out beyond your normal responsibilities to achieve the desired result. Explain the role that you played in resolving this challenge.
Your responses will help us understand your experience and how you drive and lead projects to create recognizable impact for our customers and employees. Applications without this specific motivation will not be reviewed.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
If you want to apply for the B-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.
Disclaimer external recruitment agencies
External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. In addition, a recruitment agency can only submit a resume when invited by a Talent Acquisition Specialist to join the search for a right candidate. All unsolicited resumes sent to us will be considered property of ABN AMRO BANK N.V. In this case, ABN AMRO will not be held liable to pay a placement fee.

match.llm_rationale: The resume shows strong technical skills in GenAI, including experience with agentic AI solutions and production deployments, but has only 3+ years of experience versus the required 5+ years for leadership, and lacks explicit project leadership experience matching the role's emphasis on managing teams and business collaboration.

title: Senior Data Scientist (GenAI) DFC
description:
At a glance
Are you ready to join one of ABN AMRO's largest and fastest-growing data science teams dedicated to Detecting Financial Crime (DFC)? As a Data Scientist at DFC you’ll work on socially relevant projects with significant impact on the financial system's stability and security. All the while you get the chance to work on the frontier of Data Science innovation.
Your job
You'll be joining a vibrant group of about 35 Data Science Experts in the Innovation team of our Detecting Financial Crime in Amsterdam. You’ll collaborate closely with DFC teams and other departments, bringing fresh perspectives and energy to our innovative projects.
In this role you’ll be transforming traditional transaction monitoring and Know-Your-Customer (KYC) processes into state-of-the-art machine learning and genAI technologies, such as NLP and LLM. This responsibility extends to continuously tailoring models adapting them to business needs and emerging financial crime threats.
Here’s some insights in your new role:
Design and develop genAI models for transaction monitoring and KYC processes.
Collaborate with business units, Compliance, and DFC analysts to enhance financial crime detection.
Tailor data-driven models to evolving business needs and financial crime threats.
Interpret model results and effectively communicate findings to stakeholders.
Working environment
The Detecting Financial Crime – Data Science Innovation Team at ABN AMRO is a key player in the fight against financial crime, including activities such as money laundering, terrorist financing, and tax evasion. As one of the largest and fastest-growing data science teams within the organization, our department is dedicated to developing innovative solutions that maintain the integrity of the financial system.  Situated in Amsterdam, this vibrant group of over 35 Data Science Experts collaborates closely with various departments and DFC teams. We strive for growth and improvement, offering an exciting environment for innovation.
Our work is highly visible and impactful, drawing attention from senior management, national regulators, and society, underscoring their crucial role as gatekeepers of financial stability. By harnessing a combination of expertise and innovative approaches, we try to ensure that we detect all perpetrators in the banking system. Through this work, we align the interests of clients, the bank, and society, making its mission both professionally rewarding and socially relevant.
Your profile
You have a master’s degree or PhD in computer science/data science or related field and you have at least 5 years work experience as a Data Scientist and at least 2 years work experience with GenAI.
You have experience with working with unstructured data and transforming that data for GenAI application design.
You have experience with Azure AI services and trade-offs of different GenAI models.
You have experience with exposure to frameworks and concepts relevant to GenAI or Natural language processing (any experience in e.g. RAG, DSPy, langchain, agents, NLP classification problems, topic modeling etc.).
You are familiar with recent developments in machine learning, knowledge of IT architecture and an experienced Python programming, preferably also skilled in PySpark and other technologies like GIT, Azure.
Having working experience within the KYC/CDD/AML domain is beneficial.
Located in the Netherlands.
Characteristics:
You have a sense for business, and understand how Data Science and genAI specifically can be used to deliver value.
You know how to get support for your ideas and advice.
You are pragmatic and value-focussed.
You are able to communicate effectively with technical and business counterparts.
You are a teamplayer that requires minimal steering to be productive.
You love grow individually & as a team.
You have a pro-active mindset and are a self-starter.
Of course, this is our view on the matter! If you don’t check all the boxes, but are confident you are a great fit tell us why!
We are offering
At DFC, you’ll be working in a driven and dynamic environment, where people work hard based on a shared vision and objective. The DFC department is characterised by a non-hierarchical structure, short lines of communication, a focus on deliverables and close teamwork with other departments.
Although we make high demands of our work, we never lose sight of the human dimension because we believe that the success of our mission depends on it. In this work context, we’ll offer you the opportunity to work on your professional and personal development offering custom growth for people of all seniority.
Apart from that we offer:
The gross monthly salary displayed above is based on a 36-hour work week, including vacation pay and benefit budget.
The Benefit Budget is 11% of your salary. The Benefit Budget allows you to acquire additional employment benefits. If you make no purchases or reservations in the Benefit Shop in a given month, you are paid one twelfth of your Benefit Budget that month.
Five weeks of vacation per year. You have the option to purchase an additional four weeks per year.
Personal development Budget of € 1,000 per year, which you can accumulate up to € 3,000.
A hybrid way of working with on average only two work days in the office.
An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands.
A more than excellent pension scheme.
Interested?
Ready for the next stage of your career? We look forward to your application! Any question around the vacancy, please reach-out to the assigned recruiter.
Process & Timeline:
Vacancy is open for applications till
17-04-2026
Final selection around beginning of May
Interview 1 upon invite first week of May
Home-case + Case interview upon continuance
Final interview with HR and Management
Offer
Onboarding, including a full background screening by our security department (Integrity Sensitive Position-screening)
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable

Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
At a glance
Do you want to shape how AI transforms the banking industry? Are you passionate about advanced machine learning, LLMs, and intelligent data products? Do you get energy from turning complex business challenges into AI‑driven solutions?
Then this is your opportunity to help accelerate the AI revolution within I&T / Change Management & Consultancy / Enterprise Enabling Data Domain!
Your job
As our Data Scientist, you play a key role in realising the ABN AMRO strategy by designing, developing, and bringing AI‑powered solutions into production. You work with modern tooling, large and complex datasets, and cloud-based platforms to create solutions that deliver tangible business value.
You identify opportunities where AI can enhance processes, support strategic decision‑making, and unlock new ways of working. You collaborate with stakeholders to understand problems and translate them into scalable AI products. The departments see the data scientists as the expert on this subject, so that is what you will be for them.
Examples of your responsibilities
Designing, training, and evaluating advanced machine learning, including generative AI and LLM‑based solution
Exploring and applying complex datasets, metadata structures, knowledge graphs, and unstructured data
Developing scalable, production ready, AI products using Azure, Databricks, and Spark
Translating business questions into AI opportunities and communicating insights clearly
Driving innovation by experimenting with advanced AI capabilities such as generative AI, agentic systems, vector‑based retrieval, and automated model development
Collaborating with Machine Learning Engineers, Data Engineers, Data Analysts, and Domain Experts to embed AI into business and IT processes
Advising stakeholders on responsible AI, data quality, explainability and model governance
Working environment
You will join the Enterprise Enabling Data Domain, a cross‑functional team that delivers Business Insights and AI solutions for a wide range of stakeholders across the I&T organisation and beyond.
We work with cutting‑edge technology and encourage innovation, experimentation, and continuous improvement. The atmosphere is informal, open, and focused on learning — mistakes are part of success.
The team consists of around 18 professionals: Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers, working closely together on projects that matter. Collaboration with departments like CDO, COO, P&T, Procurement, CIO's, CISO and bank‑wide programs is part of your daily work.
Your profile
You are curious, analytical, and passionate about AI. You can translate complex questions into practical, high‑impact AI solutions. You understand both the technical side of AI and how to turn it into value for stakeholders.
You enjoy working with others, communicate clearly, and are always eager to explore new AI capabilities. You are excited to share your insights with multiple stakeholders and data specialists!
This is on your resume:
Proven experience with machine learning, LLMs and generative or agentic AI
Hands‑on with Azure Machine Learning, Databricks, and Spark
Proficient in Python with experience in modern ML/AI libraries and frameworks (e.g. PyTorch, scikit-learn, and related tooling)
Experience with MLOps, model deployment, monitoring, and automated pipelines
Experience with innovation‑driven projects involving emerging AI techniques
Up‑to‑date knowledge of modern AI/ML research, tooling and best practices
Fluent in English
We also like you to have:
Strong stakeholder management and advisory skills
Ownership mindset and a passion for producing high‑quality, production‑ready solutions
Enjoyment in working in a multicultural and interdisciplinary environment
Understanding of responsible AI principles, fairness, transparency, and governance
Experience working with big data ecosystems
We are offering
At ABN AMRO, the success of our organisation depends on the quality and diversity of our people. We foster a culture of Care, Collaboration, and Courage. We encourage innovation and inclusive teamwork — the foundation of truly surprising insights. To ensure that everyone at ABN AMRO can develop their talents, we encourage an inclusive culture in which all colleagues feel engaged and appreciated:
The opportunity to grow into the best AI professional you can be
Personal development budget of €1.000 per year
Flexible and hybrid working options
A public transportation pass or travel budget
Work with leading AI technologies in a modern data environment
The freedom and responsibility to own your solutions end‑to‑end
Interested?
Please express your interest by applying online with your resume and a motivational letter. For questions, contact Yasmine de Jeu – Head of AI CoE - if you have any questions via email: yasmine.de.jeu@nl.abnamro.com.
This vacancy closes on Friday, 10 April 2026, and applications can be submitted until this date.

Machine Learning Scientist
At Adyen, we are the financial technology platform of choice for the world’s leading companies. The Insights team is at the core of this platform, providing the world’s largest merchants with the data and analytics they need to optimize their payment performance. Within this, our Proactive Diagnostics initiative acts as a proactive guard for merchant revenue, closing the loop between detecting an anomaly and providing a clear path to rectification.
We operate at the intersection of Big Data and actionable intelligence. By leveraging Adyen’s global payment flow, we apply advanced statistical models and Causal Inference to not only detect performance drops but to explain the "why" behind them. We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to help us architect the next generation of our diagnostic engine.
In this role, you will:
Build
– Design and scale production-ready ML models to identify anomalies across millions of traffic permutations. You will own the end-to-end lifecycle, from feature engineering within our Big Data ecosystem (Spark/SparkStreaming) to building the internal infrastructure—such as our FastAPI-based labeling service on Kubernetes—needed to generate high-quality ground truth for supervised learning.
Discover
– Move beyond simple detection to build automated root-cause analysis. You will develop logic that translates complex statistical signals into actionable recommendations, helping merchants understand exactly how to optimize their setup.
Scale
– Transition our diagnostic capabilities from daily reporting to near-real-time velocity. You will build the observability layer to track model drift and integrity, ensuring our signals remain the "Gold Standard" for the industry.
Collaborate
– Work at the heart of a product-driven team. You will sit close to our users, gathering continuous feedback to ensure our technical solutions solve real-world business friction and drive product adoption.
Who You Are:
You have 4+ years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or Data Scientist (Anomaly Detection, Time-Series, or Signal Processing).
You have an Engineering-First mindset. You treat ML code like production code and are comfortable managing your own deployments and infrastructure.
You are proficient in Python and Big Data frameworks (PySpark, Airflow, Hadoop, Kafka).
Experience with SparkStreaming/Flink, Docker and Kubernetes is a plus.
You have a strong interest in Causal Inference - you want to prove
why
something happened, not just
that
it happened.
You are a pragmatic problem solver. You prioritize business impact and reliability over model complexity, choosing the right tool for the job to ship solutions that work today.
You are proactively taking the lead in projects, from ideation to deployment. You have experience working with a wide range of stakeholders and can clearly communicate complex outcomes to a wide range of audiences.
You can confidently work in a product team with demanding stakeholders, are able to communicate effectively and have the ability to drive the team’s roadmap, alongside the product and engineering leadership of the team.

title: Medior Data Scientist (Python, Azure, Databricks, Spark)
description:
.
Your day is done—you’ve pushed your code, and it’s already been reviewed and merged. Now it’s time to grab some groceries on the way home. Walking through the refrigerated aisles of your local Albert Heijn store, you spot a great bargain—salad leaves marked down to 70%, thanks to a dynamic discount algorithm you helped develop, ensuring products sell before expiration and avoiding waste.
As you walk towards the bakery department, the aroma of freshly baked goods fills the air. You take pride in knowing that your optimized baking plan is behind the freshness and quality of these products. Stepping out onto the street you realize that it’s not just your local store—these algorithms run in more than a thousand stores across the Netherlands and Belgium.
Does a job with such tangible impact at such scale motivates you? Do you want to use your skills to solve a wide set of practical and challenging problems? We’re looking for curious, collaborative people who care about impact and learning to join and strengthen our Operations Analytics and Data Science team!
As Medior Data Scientist you:
Improve our algorithms through analysis, modelling and experimentation
Design, build, test, and productionize algorithms in collaboration with engineers and analysts
Work on ETL pipelines to make sure we work with up-to-date and correct data
Convey complex ideas to different audiences in an effective manner
Work in multidisciplinary product teams closely to our business stakeholders
Share your knowledge with and learn from other team members
Work with colleagues who value open discussion, constructive code reviews, and diverse perspectives.
We’re looking for candidates with:
A Master in Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Econometrics, Computer Science, or other relevant studies
Knowledge of Machine Learning and Applied Statistical techniques
Strong knowledge of Python
2-5 years of relevant experience
Experience using modern software development practices (IDEs, Unit testing, Version Control, CI/CD, etc.)
Experience with generative AI–assisted coding (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, or similar) and the ability to prompt effectively, assess suggestions critically, and uphold security and quality standards.
Experience with Cloud Platforms, Databricks and Spark is a plus
Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role, we encourage you to apply.
You can also read more about some of the technical details of our work on our blog:
https://blog.ah.technology
What we offer:
A salary up to €75K gross per year, including holiday allowance (8%). On top of that you will receive a profit share
25,5 vacation days
A budget choice in which you can decide whether you spend it on extra days off (+19,5 days), discounts or pay-outs
Courses from our Albert Heijn TOP Academy
Hybrid working (2–3 days in the office) and flexible hours
An excellent pension plan with a personal contribution of 6.2%
A travel allowance for traveling to and from Zaandam
Attractive discounts on various insurance policies
A MacBook
and an iPhone
10% discount on your grocery shopping in our Albert Heijn stores, up to €300 per year
We find it important that you can be yourself at Albert Heijn in an environment that is open to different perspectives, an environment where we are curious and appreciate each other.
We’re Albert Heijn, food retail reinventors.
🚀
Albert Heijn goes way back. More than 135 years, to be exact. So for millions of people, food shopping is synonymous with the Albert Heijn brand. We didn’t achieve this with a complacent, ‘hey, if it works today, it’ll work tomorrow’ approach, but by continually rethinking our propositions and staying plugged in to customer demands. More than 125,000 colleagues? Check. Over 1,250 stores in the Netherlands and Flanders? Check. The leading online food retail platform? Check, we build, run and love it.
Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
The world around us has changed, and significantly at that. These changes have impact on the lives of our customers, colleagues and partners. And therefore, also on Albert Heijn. We’re moving towards a new world with new opportunities and challenges. Albert Heijn wants more people to eat fresher, healthier and more sustainable food. Eating brings us together, keeps us healthier and contributes positively to the world. Our mission is: Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
Albert Heijn the Netherlands is B Corp certified. This underscores our purpose, through which we aim to contribute to a healthier, more social, and sustainable society. B Corp certification is a designation that a business is meeting high standards of verified performance, accountability and transparency.
Ready to contribute to our mission?
Do you want to play an active role in changing the food retail landscape as we know it? Then hop on board and join us on our expedition. Would you like to learn more first, before you apply to Albert Heijn? We’re happy to tell you everything about this vacancy! An assessment might be part of our selection process.
Apply

Why do you think you will be successful in this position and what kind of technical skills do you bring with you for this role?
What is one of your personal strengths and one skill you wish to improve or develop?

.

This pioneering initiative combines cutting-edge AI research with practical applications, focusing on creating tailored solutions that redefine how travelers plan, book, and experience their journeys. Role Description: In this role, you will lead a team of Machine Learning Scientists and Engineers dedicated to building, training, and deploying cutting-edge Generative AI models. You are expected to be the focal point for all technical aspects, make sure your team members deliver on their tasks, and work together with other stakeholders to define and shape the roadmap of our products. You will work independently and will also be responsible for making technical decisions within your team. When it comes to management, your expertise in handling people will motivate and inspire them to reach outstanding success! You should have experience in developing people. You will mentor and coach your team while working closely with a Product Manager. Expertise in the engineering aspects of deploying LLMs at scale with minimal latency. This includes optimizing model performance, scalability, and efficiency to meet the demands of real-time, high-traffic applications. Define and communicate the technical vision and strategy for LLM-related initiatives, ensuring alignment with company goals and customer needs. Translate business problems into viable, reliable and robust ML and AI solutions, accounting for constraints of the production environment. Monitor product health, performance and business impact and act accordingly when requirements are not met. Drive, coach and mentor others through evidence and clear communication, explaining advanced technical concepts in simpler terms. Continuously evolve your craft. Keep up to date with industry and academic standard methodologies, periodically explore new technologies, introduce them to the machine learning community and promote their application in areas where they can generate impact. Actively contribute to Machine Learning at Booking.com through training, exploration of new technologies, interviewing, onboarding and mentoring colleagues. Push for improvements, scaling and extending machine learning tooling and infrastructure, collaborating with central teams. Qualifications & Skills: Leadership Experience: At least 4 years of experience leading ML teams in Natural Language Processing (NLP) or Generative AI (GenAI) domains, with a proven ability to guide teams in achieving impactful results. Experience on multiple machine learning facets: working with large data sets, model development, statistics, experimentation, data visualization, optimization, software development. Experience collaborating cross functionally in the development of machine learning products (e.g. Developers, UX specialists, Product Managers, etc.). Strong working knowledge of Python, Java, Kafka, Hadoop, SQL, and Spark or similar technologies. Working experience with version control systems. Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal. Leading by example, gaining respect through actions, not your title. Developing your team and motivating them to achieve their goals. title: Data scientist Business Banking NL
description:
Data Scientist Business Banking NL
Would you like to shape how ING Business Banking understands and influences customer behaviour across the end‑to‑end customer journey? Are you energized by complex data, advanced analytics, and turning insights into real business impact? Then this role is for you!
About CoE Pricing Business Banking
The Center of Expertise Pricing Business Banking is part of Business Development, a central BBNL department working for all business banking products and segments. With the strategic product-price positioning, we impact the results of the Domestic Bank Business Banking Netherlands, and customer behaviour, credit and market risk, and perception of our customers and society. As CoE Pricing we have a holistic view on Business Banking products and segments covering savings, payments and lending for small to midsized corporates.
Customer behavioural modelling and Insights Chapter
You will be part of the Customer behavioural modelling and Insights Chapter. This is a newly established squad designed to understand our customers’ behaviour. The focus areas are on behaviour modelling, customer segmentation and price elasticity. You will have the opportunity to build analytical solutions from the ground up, shape frameworks and best practices from the ground up.
Role and responsibilities
Develop advanced analytics solutions and models to drive data-informed commercial decisions across Business Banking.
Collaborate with subject matter experts to bring models into production.
Take a leading role in solution development to support business in understanding customer behaviour through data.
Actively engage with stakeholders.
How to succeed
We hire smart people like you for your potential. Our biggest expectation is that you’ll stay curious. Keep learning. Take on responsibility. In return, we’ll back you to develop into an even more awesome version of yourself.
To fullfill this role succesfully, you need to be:
Proactive and result driven.
Highly motivated to solve complex problems with a continuous learning mindset.
Comfortable in dynamic multi-matrix environments.
Enjoy sharing knowledge and helping teammates grow.
Also you bring:
At least 5 years of experience developing advanced analytics solutions, ideally in the financial sector. Demonstrated ability to solve business problems and develop innovative models or data-driven products.
Proven track record of hands-on experience in multiple data science projects. Broad experience with end-to-end project implementation, including all steps from data extraction to modelling and deployment.
Affinity with panel data / time series models, linear and non-linear regressions, time series analysis, clustering, and hypothesis testing.
